original_abstract:
In the rapidly evolving landscape of healthcare, the integration of deep learning (DL) with medical image analysis has become a cornerstone for enhancing diagnostic precision and operational efficiency. By enabling the real-time processing of vast and intricate datasets, these advanced DL techniques offer insights that significantly improve patient outcomes. This comprehensive review systematically examines the latest DL methodologies designed to overcome persistent challenges in medical imaging. Specifically, we categorize state-of-the-art approaches into five primary groups: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models, and hybrid architectures.

Through a rigorous analysis of the existing literature, we explore the underlying principles, inherent advantages, and practical limitations of these methodologies, while also documenting the simulation environments and datasets utilized in their development. Our findings indicate that Python remains the predominant programming language for implementing these models, reflecting its robust ecosystem for scientific computing. Notably, a surge in publications during 2021 highlights the current momentum and contemporaneous nature of this research field. Beyond summarizing technical milestones, this review addresses the critical hurdles—such as data privacy, model interpretability, and generalizability—that currently impede the widespread clinical adoption of DL. By synthesizing performance metrics across various studies (including accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F-score, and computational complexity), this study serves as a strategic roadmap for future research aimed at advancing the frontier of intelligent medical healthcare.
humanized_abstract:
Healthcare is undergoing tremendous transformation and deep learning (DL) in medical image analysis is one of the key drivers in this transformation. They facilitate rapid diagnostics, high accuracy and most importantly improved patient outcomes through real time processing of large amount of complex data. In this review, we shall be discussing recent advancements in Deep learning that help in resolving many of the unsolved problems in the field of medical image analysis. The literatures were classified under five main groups namely: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, and hybrid models.

Practically all recent studies dealing with the application of deep learning (DL) methods in medical healthcare, with a specific focus on imaging analysis, have employed extensive literature reviews. Although the underlying theories, unique characteristics, and corresponding limitations of the various methods are described in detail, as well as the corresponding simulation systems and databases, the overall focus is on how the corresponding algorithms are implemented. It is confirmed that almost all existing researches implemented their corresponding algorithms by utilizing Python, a set of features that strengthen its advantage in the scientific computing field. The large amount of papers published in 2021 also proves the pros and cons of the field of researches. Other than summarizing the progresses in the research, it also addresses the challenges encountered in the practical application of DL in medical sector, such as protection of medical privacy, explanation of medical decisions, and generalization of the models, and provides a comprehensive overview based on the corresponding evaluation indexes, such as accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F-score, and efficiency in the related researches. It can be a valuable reference for the medical sector to promote the developments in intelligent medical healthcare.
